objective: Research and produce a validated plan for deploying the Veritas veritas-example harness to Modal (serverless compute platform), covering container image compatibility, secrets/config handling, and mapping of existing CLI verbs to Modal app entrypoints.

specialists.focus: Modal's deployment model (functions, images, secrets, volumes) and how it maps onto the harness's existing bun-based Dockerfile/docker-compose setup
Synthesize findings into a deployment plan that preserves the harness's config/secret-resolution and reproducibility invariants

phases.description: Research Modal's official documentation for container image requirements, function/app deployment model, and secrets management
Assess compatibility of the veritas-example Dockerfile and bun runtime with Modal's supported base images and build process
Identify how ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and other config values in src/config/ (env-var resolution, local.json) should be provided via Modal's secrets mechanism
Map existing CLI commands (bun run build, bun test, bun run dev start/eval/digest, doctor) to Modal app entrypoints, noting which are one-shot jobs vs long-running
Document risks from Modal's ephemeral filesystem for mission/evidence artifacts and how this interacts with the reproducibility invariant enforced by verify-claims

successCriteria: Verify the resulting deployment plan reproduces via `bun run verify-claims` with zero unreproduced claims
Confirm at least 3 Modal-specific deployment risks (image build, secrets injection, ephemeral filesystem) are documented with mitigations
Reproduce a complete entrypoint mapping covering 100 percent of the harness's existing CLI verbs (build, test, doctor, dev start/eval/digest)
